title: Turai Sabzi with Roti
description: Soft ridge-gourd sabzi cooked without onion or garlic, plated with rotis as a light complete meal.
explanation: Turai (ridge gourd) is mild, watery, and perfect for evenings when you want vegetables without a heavy curry. This version uses hing and tomato so it stays Jain and no-onion/no-garlic.
Roti is included so the recipe is a full main, not only a side sabzi.
ingredients: 3 cups Turai (ridge gourd), peeled and sliced
instructions: Heat oil. Add mustard seeds, then cumin and hing. Add optional chilli.
Add tomato, turmeric, coriander powder, and salt. Cook until tomato softens.
Cover and cook on low–medium heat until turai is soft and translucent, 10–12 minutes. Uncover near the end if too watery.
Finish with lemon and coriander. Serve with hot rotis.
stepNotes: Peel thick ridges; young turai needs less peeling.
If turai tastes bitter, discard it—bitterness does not cook out reliably.
Do not drown the pan in water; turai releases moisture.
Soft, slightly glossy pieces are done; mushy turai loses character.
A spoon of grated coconut at the end gives a gentle South Indian lean.

variationSections.title: Vegetable swaps
items: Use lauki or tinda with the same tempering.
Add a few soaked chana dal pieces cooked with the sabzi for more body.
Mix in chopped spinach in the last minutes.

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: Millet roti for a gluten-free variation.
Add a small bowl of plain dal when you need more protein.
Serve with cut fruit for a lighter evening plate.
